name: Robert Blackwell
abstract: Robert Alexander "Bumps" Blackwell (May 23, 1918 – March 9, 1985) was an American bandleader, songwriter, arranger, and record producer, best known for his work overseeing the early hits of Little Richard, as well as mentoring Ray Charles, Quincy Jones, Ernestine Anderson, Lloyd Price, Sam Cooke, Herb Alpert, Larry Williams, and Sly and …

about_quotes.speaker: Holman Bill
about_quotes.evidence: A guy named Bumps Blackwell did it. ... So I put it up to this guy, and he said sure, no problem. So this was my first effort, to get Jimmy Rowles with four horns and do an album.
about_quotes.doc_title: Holman Bill — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Lawrence Cotton
about_quotes.evidence: we made a recording out there with Bumps Blackwell with Slim. We was in the studio out there for specialty.
